ab: ko ’avalavalakili ka ’iyakay kiname Kongadavane, ko patekedriname ka la ’aela kiname mwa Takaw sikace.
ch: 小時候我們在多納，但是長大就搬到了高雄，在都市裡生活。
en: When we were young, our family lived in Kongadavane. After I started school, we moved to Kaohsiung and have been living in the city ever since.

ab: ko mwalinga daigake ka la kica’oane kake na tolwanini na kacaisyane, lasoigo’o kake Taiwan kacaisyane na ma’ielela na bengka.
ch: 上了大學以後，我修了一堂關於原住民文化的課程，瞭解到臺灣原住民族有各式各樣豐富的文化。
en: After I went to college, I took a course on aboriginal culture and learned that the aboriginal people in Taiwan have various and rich cultures.

ab: nikwani nakay cicavili hoyasemi kake si kaka la ’abee kiname mwa tacekecekelane kiboboo na maodrange. taigo’o na bengkaname mya.
ch: 因此，今年暑假時，我和哥哥回到部落訪問老人家，想要瞭解自己族群的文化。
en: So this summer, my brother and I went back to our tribe to visit the elderly, hoping to understand the culture of our own ethnic group.

ab: maodrange ka watamavaolonga na cavilini, makasi cengelimade ka amiya na ’athabaela.
ch: 老人家已經八十歲了，但是看起來還像中年人。
en: The old man is already eighty years old, but he still looks like a middle-aged man.

ab: la kiboboo kake nyane maniigo’iso na baka mya, maygili ’apobaka na wadrekadrekane, waigo’o na wapakikisane. wakokaomane si wa’amolrilrikane.
ch: 我問他會哪些語言，他的魯凱語說得很流利，也會說閩南語、日語、英語。
en: I asked him which languages he knew, and he was fluent in Rukai, as well as Hokkien, Japanese and English.

ab: ko boboilinga lasoigo’o kake ko ’avalavalakini ka kyaca’oane na wakokaomane, palangini ka pakisa, si ta’akomolanini ka la paygoigo’ane na taligaykoko, nikwani igo’o ’angiyadraw na ma’akabakanga.
ch: 我問他才知道，原來他小時候就學日本話，太太是漢人，而他工作上認識許多外國人，所以他會說這麼多語言。
en: I asked him to find out that he learned Japanese when he was a child, his wife is an Chinese, and he knows many foreign at work, so he can speak so many languages.

ab: ko mabakabakainame ka lapatolotolnga kiname taiyanini na walopo.
ch: 我們訪談的時候，老人家談論了許多關於狩獵的傳統知識。
en: During our interview, the old man talked about a lot of traditional knowledge about hunting.

ab: itakaigo’ane kiname bicingi, makasi syakay kiname na so-ci lo-ing, la doo la kiname pa’ingcege biciingi.
ch: 雖然我們沒有辦法完全聽懂，但是我們有用手機錄影，可以再仔細聆聽。
en: Although we could’t fully understand it, we could record it on our cell phones and listen to it more.

ab: ’avalavalakili ka itakaigo’ane kake na bengka kacaisyane, ’angi ka’ino kake taakacaisyanili.
ch: 小時候我不瞭解自己族群的文化，對於自己是原住民有點不好意思。
en: When I was young, I didn’t understand the culture of my ethnic group, and I was a little embarrassed that I was an aborigine.

ab: madrawininga igo’ili la kabeabeenga kake, ’angipakileve thakongadavane kake mya.
ch: 瞭解愈多以後，愈覺得自己身為多納人，是很獨特的，也很值得驕傲。
en: The more I know, the more I feel that being a Kongadavane is unique and something to be proud of.
